title: Review P1 wave: prefix keep-set, strict prune flags, fail-closed workers, C1 re-read
action: Fix the four P1s from the correctness review: (1) keep decisions move from suffix-stem mapping to prefix matching against a keep set (active worker cells + all non-capped cells), so dotted cell ids can never be mis-stemmed; empty-stem names are never candidates. (2) prune rejects unknown flags, and --dry-run is a hard error on every other verb. (3) state.workers that is not an array fails closed before any deletion. (4) keep set re-read immediately before the destructive loop (C1); swarming doc wording no longer claims race-safety. Pin all of it with tests (P2).

truths: alpha.out10.log survives when alpha.out10 is an active worker cell
worker prune --dryrun exits non-zero and deletes nothing
worker clear --dry-run exits non-zero and mutates nothing
workers as a non-array object exits non-zero with zero deletions
subdirectory contents and empty-stem names are never touched

artifacts.substantive: readPruneKeepSet + keptByPruneKeepSet prefix rules, strict flag validation, top-level dry-run guard
expanded fixtures (dotted id, empty stems, subdir) + 2 new checks, exact-set dry-run assert

prohibitions: Do not write state.json from the prune path

trace.outcome: All four review P1s fixed: prefix keep-set (active workers + non-capped cells) replaces suffix-stem mapping; prune rejects unknown flags and --dry-run is a hard error on mutating verbs; non-array state.workers fails closed; keep set re-read before the destructive loop (C1). P2 test gaps closed: dotted-id, empty-stem, subdir fixtures; exact-set dry-run assert; semantic-corruption check; unknown-flag checks.
